Tests if an uninitialized variable is undefined.

# Purpose
This code is a simple script that demonstrates the initialization and default value of an uninitialized variable in JavaScript. The variable `x` is declared but not assigned a value, which means it holds the value `undefined`. The `assert` function checks that `x` is strictly equal to `void 0`, which is another way to represent `undefined` in JavaScript. This script provides narrow functionality, focusing on verifying the behavior of uninitialized variables.
